concerns.text: FR-006 mandates 'System MUST fit a Generalized Additive Model (GAM)... If GAM fails, use segmented regression'. Tasks T030/T031 invert this by making 'Parametric 1/sqrt(k)' the primary method and GAM secondary. This is a semantic weakening of the functional requirement's algorithmic mandate.

FR-001 requires acquiring 'at least 50 real-world meta-analyses'. T012/T014 implement an immediate fallback to simulation if download fails, effectively allowing the project to satisfy the task list with synthetic data while bypassing the real-world acquisition mandate. This violates the spec's core data source constraint.

SC-003 and FR-007 require identifying stabilization within '±2% of the nominal 95% level'. T033 instructs reading this target from `config.py` but provides no task to define this value. Since the spec marks the target as '[deferred]' in SC-003, the task is unimplementable without a prior definition step, creating a silent drift from the spec's explicit 95% reference.

T014 conflates 'chunked processing logic' (a prerequisite for real data) with 'simulation fallback' (a contingency). Merging these distinct concerns violates the separation of concerns required to verify the real-data path independently, risking the silent relaxation of the real-data acquisition requirement.
